title: Dr. Soliman Fakeeh Hospital in Jeddah receives Saudi Arabia’s first Gold Patient Safety Certificate
summary: The hospital met all required standards set by the Saudi Patient Safety Center, reflecting strong compliance with structured safety protocols and system-wide quality controls across its clinical a ...
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
SAUDI ARABIA—Dr. Soliman Fakeeh Hospital in Jeddah has achieved a major milestone after receiving the Gold Level Patient Safety Standards Certificate in the first cycle of the national program.

The hospital is the first and only facility in the Kingdom to secure this recognition in the current cycle, marking a significant step in its quality and safety journey.

The certification program is overseen by the Saudi Patient Safety Center (SPSC), the national body responsible for advancing patient safety standards and strengthening healthcare quality across Saudi Arabia in line with the Health Sector Transformation Program and Vision 2030.

Comprehensive evaluation across key safety domains

The Gold Level certification follows a rigorous assessment based on 10 patient safety domains and 250 criteria.

The evaluation covered leadership and governance, patient engagement, workforce competence, clinical care processes, medication safety, infection prevention, risk management, incident reporting systems, and continuous quality improvement.

The hospital met all required standards set by the Saudi Patient Safety Center, reflecting strong compliance with structured safety protocols and system-wide quality controls across its clinical and operational departments.

Strengthening a culture of safe and patient-centered care

Hospital leadership said the achievement reflects a long-standing commitment to embedding safety, transparency, and continuous improvement into everyday care.

Physicians, nurses, and support staff worked collectively to strengthen patient-centered practices and enhance reliability across services.

The recognition also highlights the hospital’s focus on creating safer care environments while improving communication, accountability, and patient involvement in clinical decisions.

Broader quality and international accreditations

In addition to the national recognition, Dr. Soliman Fakeeh Hospital – Jeddah continues to expand its portfolio of quality achievements.

It previously recorded an exceptional 110% evaluation score from the Council of Health Insurance (CHI), alongside a Gold Level Award for its implementation of Patient-Reported Outcome Measures (PROMs) under the Value-Based Healthcare Program.

Internationally, the hospital holds Planetree Gold Certification for Person-Centered Care, reflecting its emphasis on compassionate, patient-focused services.

 It is also ranked among the Best Specialized Hospitals in the Middle East and has been named the Best Private Hospital in Saudi Arabia for five consecutive years by Newsweek.

Furthermore, it has received accreditation for 14 Centers of Excellence from the Surgical Review Corporation (SRC).

Leadership perspective on the achievement

Dr. Mazen Fakeeh, President of Fakeeh Care Group, said the recognition reflects a deeply rooted culture that prioritizes patient safety and healthcare quality at every level of the organization.

He noted that the milestone demonstrates the hospital’s commitment to empowering healthcare teams and applying global best practices aligned with national healthcare transformation goals.

The hospital continues to strengthen its systems for patient safety, clinical quality, and service innovation.

In recent organizational developments, it has maintained its focus on enhancing patient experience, advancing quality frameworks, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives across its care network, reinforcing its position within Saudi Arabia’s evolving healthcare landscape.
